title: The FASER experiment at the Large Hadron Collider
abstract: The FASER experiment is located in the Large Hadron Collider (LHC) complex at CERN, 480 m downstream of the ATLAS collision point and aligned with the beam-collision-axis. The experiment was designed to search for light, weakly-interacting new-particles which could be produced in the LHC collisions, and, for the first-time, to study high-energy neutrinos of all flavours originating at a particle collider. This review article presents the status of FASER up to early-2026. This includes details of the FASER detector design, operation, performance and physics results, as well as briefly mentioning upgrades that have been installed since the start of FASER. In addition, future plans for the experiment are detailed.
comment: 56 pages. Submitted to Progress in Particle and Nuclear Physics as a review article
